question: What is the best way to handle independent data requests to avoid waterfall latency?

options.text: Move all requests to client `useEffect` so server waits are eliminated.
options.feedback: Client fetching may worsen UX and does not inherently remove sequential dependencies.

options.text: Run them in parallel with `Promise.all` instead of sequential awaits.
options.feedback: Correct: parallel execution removes unnecessary waiting between unrelated requests.

options.text: Disable Next.js prefetch so requests can complete one by one.
options.feedback: Prefetch behavior is separate and does not solve backend request waterfalls.

options.text: Force all requests into one long chain to preserve deterministic ordering.
options.feedback: Ordering is unnecessary for independent calls and increases total response time.

whyQuestion: Why do waterfall requests increase page latency?

whyOptions.text: Because parallel requests are blocked by the JavaScript event loop.
whyOptions.feedback: I/O requests can run concurrently; event loop does not serialize network completion.

whyOptions.text: Because SSR requires exactly one fetch per route by framework rule.
whyOptions.feedback: SSR supports multiple fetches; architecture decisions determine request strategy.

whyOptions.text: Because Next.js image optimization disables concurrent API communication.
whyOptions.feedback: Image optimization is unrelated to parallel API request execution patterns.

whyOptions.text: Because each request starts only after the previous one completes.
whyOptions.feedback: Exactly: sequential dependency accumulates waiting time across every network hop.
